items.headline: Nine new homes proposed for Old Trace Road subdivision
items.summary: The City Council will consider a proposal to subdivide a vacant 1-acre lot into nine single-family homes plus seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. This would add 16 residential units to the Residential Estate zone area.

items.headline: Supermicro invests $2.5M in San José equipment expansion
items.summary: Tech company Supermicro is installing new equipment at its Rock Avenue facility in a $2.5 million project. This represents significant manufacturing or operations investment in San José's tech sector.

items.headline: City Council approves financing for new Public Safety Building
items.summary: Mountain View is moving forward with a new public safety building, securing lease revenue bonds to fund the project. This represents a significant capital investment in the city's emergency services infrastructure.

items.headline: Council to review Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan
items.summary: The city is developing a comprehensive plan to improve biodiversity and urban tree canopy across Mountain View. The draft will be reviewed at the April 28 study session, potentially affecting how the city manages green space and environmental resilience.

items.headline: Zingsters restaurant/business opens on University Avenue
items.summary: A new business called Zingsters is opening in downtown Palo Alto at 220 University Avenue, as evidenced by approval of their storefront LED sign. This represents new commercial activity in the downtown core.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed: 67 homes plus retail near Speedy's Tacos
items.summary: Sunnyvale is considering a 4.22-acre mixed-use project that would add 55 townhomes and 12 apartments alongside 18,535 square feet of retail space, including a preserved Speedy's Tacos location. The project requires subdivision into 17 lots and represents significant new housing and commercial activity for the area.

items.headline: Campbell revises business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council approved an ordinance updating business license tax rates in Campbell's municipal code, effective in the new fiscal year. The change affects how much local businesses pay in licensing fees to the city.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara approves temporary giant signs near Levi's Stadium
items.summary: Santa Clara authorized installation of large supergraphic signs on building surfaces around Levi's Stadium during FIFA World Cup 2026 events. The temporary signage will be allowed for a limited time period surrounding the special sporting events.
